<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>

# Check current state
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("Current wall boundaries:")
for r in [1, 27, 28, 40, 50, 63]:
    line = ascii_lines[r]
    transitions = []
    for c in range(63):
        if line[c] != line[c+1]:
            transitions.append((c, line[c], line[c+1]))
    if transitions:
        print(f"  Row {r}: {transitions}")

# Let me also look at the level 1 final state to understand the pattern
# Find level 1 completion frame
for i, entry in enumerate(history):
    if entry.frame.level == 1 and i > 0:
        prev_level = history[i-1].frame.level
        if prev_level != 1 or entry.frame.level == 2:
            pass  # This is the transition to level 2
# Let me find the last frame of level 1
for i in range(len(history)-1, -1, -1):
    if history[i].frame.level == 1:
        l1_frame = history[i].frame
        l1_lines = l1_frame.ascii.split('\n')
        print(f"\nLevel 1 final frame (history entry {i}):")
        for r in [1, 27, 28, 40, 50, 63]:
            line = l1_lines[r]
            transitions = []
            for c in range(63):
                if line[c] != line[c+1]:
                    transitions.append((c, line[c], line[c+1]))
            if transitions:
                print(f"  Row {r}: {transitions}")
        break
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
